user_system_path: #478: commitment age was anchored on extraction time, not the real event date. insertCommitment set made_at = now() instead of the source signal's occurred_at, so a year-old 'send filings to counsel' (source 2025-06) got a fabricated 'due in 7 days' and ranked 74-82. Fix: thread signal.occurred_at into insertCommitment as madeAtIso (fallback now() only when undated); the deterministic scorer then derives implied deadline + staleness from the real date. Backfilled live (made_at corrected to occurred_at; pool re-scored): recent real obligations lead (declined payment $346.61 @ 2026-06-17 = 88 #1), year-old legal filings collapse to 17-25. Builds on #474 consequence scoring.
allowed_files_raw: The deterministic commitment risk scorer (lib/signals/commitment-risk.ts) + its real-data regression test + control-plane. No schema, no deps, no paid calls.
forbidden_files_raw: No app/api, no schema (supabase/**), no deps (package.json/lock).
required_local_proof: npx vitest run lib/signals/__tests__/commitment-risk.test.ts && npx vitest run lib/signals/ && npm run gate:continuity && npm run typecheck && npm run build
required_browser_proof: N/A — deterministic, free scoring function; verified by recomputing real production tkg_commitments rows (the $7,199.50 waiver rises to the gem band; $2.71 credit / $21.66 subscription / $198 autopay collapse to 22). A backfill of stored risk_score applies it to the live pool owner-side.
acceptance_condition: computeCommitmentRisk scales the money signal by magnitude and collapses informational money movement, so a $7,199.50 statutory waiver structurally outranks a $2.71 statement credit / $6.50 milk frother / $21.66 subscription; real-data regression test green; lib/signals suite + typecheck + gate:continuity green.
anti_regression_checks: lib/signals suite green; only lib/signals/commitment-risk.ts changed under lib/
informational receipts (statement credit / subscription / scheduled autopay) stay in the noise tier (<40); a real declined-payment obligation stays high
stop_condition: Stop when the PR is open with deterministic + real-data proof. Owner-side: run the backfill to apply the recomputed risk_score to the live commitment pool.
next_command: Owner: merge, then run scripts/backfill-commitment-risk.ts to re-score stored commitments so the live generation pool reflects the fix. Follow-up lever: carry the same magnitude/consequence principle into the signal-side stakes scoring and the generator's positive_winner_contract so the surfaced winner is the high-consequence obligation, not a recycled do_nothing.
